$comment: Neutral shipped <sys/mman.h> (POSIX) — c14 POSIX/SQLite. The memory-mapped-I/O surface SQLite's os_unix.c uses for its mmap'd page cache: mmap/munmap + Linux mremap (resize, under SQLITE_MMAP_READWRITE). Constants PROT_READ/PROT_WRITE (page protections) + MAP_SHARED (the only map type SQLite uses — a shared file mapping) + MREMAP_MAYMOVE (the mremap flag). MAP_FAILED is the `((void*)-1)` sentinel mmap returns on failure — a pointer, so the MACROS surface (not an integer constant). Linux values (arch-invariant within linux); gated availableObjectFormats:[elf] (the WSL run target). off_t is i64 (LP64). No over-ship: exactly SQLite's mmap surface. macOS (different MAP_* values, no mremap) is a c14-macOS follow-on.

symbols.name: mmap
symbols.signature: fn(ptr<void>, u64, i32, i32, i32, i64) -> ptr<void>
